publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, canonical fill-gap, and runnable core task for Class inheritance; every item remains blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Class inheritance: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Java: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? Inheritance is appropriate whenever two classes share fields or helper code, even if the subtype rejects base operations.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. Use inheritance only for a valid substitution relationship; otherwise extract a collaborator or shared helper and compose it.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Use inheritance only for a valid substitution relationship; otherwise extract a collaborator or shared helper and compose it.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.

options.text: Use inheritance only for a valid substitution relationship; otherwise extract a collaborator or shared helper and compose it.
Inheritance is appropriate whenever two classes share fields or helper code, even if the subtype rejects base operations.

answer.explanation: Use inheritance only for a valid substitution relationship; otherwise extract a collaborator or shared helper and compose it.

items.title: Class inheritance: runnable core task
items.prompt: Read square or rectangle and dimensions. Model both as Shape subtypes and print integer area through a Shape reference.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Start from the stated input/output contract, implement the smallest language-specific operation, and run the visible boundary before changing structure.

feedback.correct: The visible and hidden Java: Beginner to Intermediate runner checks match the declared contract.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic runner check differs; inspect input parsing, boundary behavior, and exact output before retrying.

criteria.description: Compiles or parses and passes every visible and hidden deterministic runner check for the declared contract.

privateAuthorNotes: Reference solution and both tests are AI-assisted draft material. Execute in the pinned runner and obtain independent human review before any exam use.

answer.explanation: Each subtype satisfies the complete Shape area contract and initializes its own required representation.
